title: Inverted Clamp FISH Ligation Protocol
author: Benjamin Emert
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.qnjdvcn
materials: NEB Monarch PCR and DNA cleanup kit

steps.instruction: Reconstitute invertedClampFISH arms, backbones and adaptors to 400uM in nuclease free water. If the quantity of backbone oligos is very low, can reconstitute to 200uM and use twice as much for subsequent reactions.

combine reaction components to phosphorylate unmodified backbone oligos undetermined, 2x T7 DNA ligase buffer undetermined, 400uM backbone oligo undetermined, T4 polynucleotide kinase undetermined, nuclease free water If you plan to add the same invertedClampFISH arms to each backbone oligo, the phosphorylation and subsequent ligation can be done with multiple backbone oligos simultaneously. I have ligated 30 backbone probes at once, scaling up the reaction 2x-3x. Undetermined, thermal cycler lid Undetermined, thermal cycler 08:00:00, incubate PNK reaction. You probably can incubate for a shorter duration. Overnight incubation is fine too.
Combine reaction components undetermined, 400μM left arm undetermined, 400μM right arm undetermined, 400μM left adapter undetermined, 400μM right adapter
Heat reaction components to 95ºC for 5 minutes then cool slowly to 12ºC. Undetermined 00:05:00, on thermal cycler Undetermined, cool slowly (0.1ºC/sec)
Bring to room temperature then add T7 DNA ligase. Undetermined, Bring to room temperature undetermined, T7 DNA ligase
Mix reaction then centrifuge. Incubate at room temperature overnight. Undetermined 12:00:00, incubate overnight If any of the oligos contain a dye, incubate in the dark.
Column purify using NEB Monarch PCR and DNA cleanup kit according to the manufacturer's instructions. Use 1 column per 10μL ligation reactions.

Add 30 μL nuclease free water to ligation reaction. undetermined, nuclease free water Can be scaled up or down as long as 7x volume of binding buffer is added before loading column. I like to scale the volume such that 200 μL can be loaded on each column.
Add 350 μL binding buffer undetermined, binding buffer
Apply to monarch column then spin down for 1 minute. 00:01:00, Centrifuge at 16,000 x g
Remove flow-through then apply 200 μL wash buffer to column and spin down. undetermined, Monarch DNA wash buffer 00:01:00, Centrifuge at 16,000 x g
Repeat wash with 200 μL wash buffer and spin down. undetermined, Monarch DNA wash buffer 00:01:00, Centrifuge at 16,000 x g You do not need to remove flow through in between washes.
Transfer column to clean centrifuge tube then spin down to remove residual wash buffer. 00:01:00, Centrifuge at 16,000 x g
Rotate column 180º then centrifuge again. 00:01:00, Centrifuge at 16,000 x g. Probably carry-over superstition from using Qiagen columns.

Transfer to clean centrifuge tube for elution. Apply 30uL nuclease-free water to column. undetermined Can scale up or down volume to adjust final concentration. With 30 μL elution, my final concentration tends to be between 50-80ng/μL (using an absorbance constant of 33 on the nanodrop).
Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes. 00:05:00, incubate at room temperature.
Elute probes. 00:01:00, Centrifuge at 16,000 x g.
Measure concentration by nanodrop. I set the absorbance constant to 33 for ssDNA.
